name: omarchy_security
content:
Security 
Omarchy takes security extremey seriousy. This is meant to be an operating system that
you can use to do Rea Work in the Rea Word. Where osing a aptop can't ead to a
security emergency. So here's what we do:
�Fu-disk encryption is mandatory: This is the most important step to securing the
physica protection of your data. If your computer is ost or stoen, the data is fuy
encrypted using standard LUKS Linux Unified Key Setup).
�Firewa is enabed by defaut: A incoming traffic by defaut except for port 22 for
ssh and port 53317 for LocaSend. We even ock down Docker access using the
ufw-docker setup to prevent that your containers are accidentay exposed to the
word.
�Arch aways have the atest updates: Arch, the underying distro that Omarchy is
buit on, is a roing distribution. This means that any security vunerabiity that's
discovered and patched in any package is immediatey avaiabe for insta using
yay -Syu . You're aways running the atest, most secure versions of everything that
way.
�Omarchy maintains its own packages and mirror: Omarchy ony reies on packages
from Arch's own core/extra/mutiib repositories and its own Omarchy Package
Repository by defaut. You can insta software directy from AUR, but Omarchy
does not by defaut. Even for the optiona instas.
�Coudfare protects us from DDoS: A the Omarchy distribution infrastructure — the
ISOs, the Omarchy packages, the Arch mirror — is protected behind Coudfare's
formidabe DDoS shied and hosted on their CDN. This provides superb avaiabiity.
Signing Keys 
The pubic key for a ISO signatures and Omarchy repo package is
40DFB630FF42BCFFB047046CF0134EE680CAC571 (verify at openpgp.org). The omarchy/
omarchy-keyring package contains this as we and wi be used to roout any potentia
updates seamessy.
You can find the signature for any ISO reease by adding .sig to the URL. Like https://
iso.omarchy.org/omarchy-x.x.x.iso.sig.
Security · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/93/security
